sections.subheading: The passive (present continuous, present perfect simple, past continuous, past perfect simple, be going to, modals)
sections.subheading_uz: The passive (present continuous, present perfect simple, past continuous, past perfect simple, be going to, modals)
sections.subheading_ru: The passive (present continuous, present perfect simple, past continuous, past perfect simple, be going to, modals)
sections.instruction: be in the right form + past participle
sections.instruction_ru: be в нужной форме + причастие прошедшего времени

cells: _The washing-up **is being done** by my aunt._
_The invitations **have been sent** by my cousin._
_The car **was being cleaned** by my uncle._
_Our neighbours had taken the twins to the zoo._
_The twins **had been taken** to the zoo by our neighbours._
cells_uz: _Qo'shnilarimiz egizaklarni hayvonot bog'iga olib borishgan edi._
_Egizaklar qo'shnilarimiz tomonidan hayvonot bog'iga **olib borilgan edi**._

cells: _They're going to invite Phil to the party._
_Phil **is going to be invited** to the party._

_They might invite Kyle to the party._
_We should tell Jenny about the party._
_We must tell Dominic about the concert._
_We can hold the party at Jack's house._

_Kyle **might be invited** to the party._
_Jenny **should be told** about the party._
_Dominic **must be told** about the concert._
_The party **can be held** at Jack's house._
cells_uz:
_Ular Kaylni ziyofatga taklif qilishi mumkin._
_Biz Jenniga ziyofat haqida aytishimiz kerak._
_Biz Dominikga konsert haqida aytishimiz shart._
_Biz ziyofatni Jek uyida o'tkazishimiz mumkin._

_Kayl ziyofatga **taklif qilinishi mumkin**._
_Jenni ziyofat haqida **xabardor qilinishi kerak**._
_Dominik konsert haqida **xabardor qilinishi shart**._
_Ziyofat Jek uyida **o'tkazilishi mumkin**._
cells_ru:
_Они могут пригласить Кайла на вечеринку._
_Мы должны сообщить Дженни о вечеринке._
_Мы обязаны сообщить Доминику о концерте._
_Мы можем провести вечеринку у Джека._

_Кайл **может быть приглашён** на вечеринку._
_Дженни **должна быть оповещена** о вечеринке._
_Доминик **должен быть оповещён** о концерте._
_Вечеринка **может быть проведена** у Джека._

tip.label: Watch out!
tip.translation:
• Kim biror narsa qilganini ta'kidlash uchun **by** ishlatamiz.
✓ _My sister's bedroom was painted **by** my parents._ (= My parents painted my sister's bedroom.)

• Kim nimadan foydalanganini ta'kidlash uchun **with** ishlatamiz.
✓ _Soup is usually eaten **with** a spoon._ (= You usually use a spoon to eat soup.)

• Kim bajarganini aytish kerak bo'lmasa yoki bilmasak, **by** yoki **with** ishlatmaymiz.
✓ _Mrs Fisher was taken to hospital yesterday._
tip.translation_ru:
• Используем **by**, чтобы подчеркнуть, кто что-то сделал.
✓ _My sister's bedroom was painted **by** my parents._ (= My parents painted my sister's bedroom.)

• Используем **with**, чтобы подчеркнуть, что использовалось.
✓ _Soup is usually eaten **with** a spoon._ (= You usually use a spoon to eat soup.)

• Не используем **by** или **with**, когда не нужно или не известно, кто что-то сделал.
✓ _Mrs Fisher was taken to hospital yesterday._
